column2: Hostname/device that emitted the log; scopes events and supports cross-host correlation.
Emitting subsystem/component (e.g., kernel, systemd, CRON, networkd); anchors template selection and grouping.
Process ID when present; links multi-line service actions and daemon messages.
High-level semantic bucket (service/kernel_net/network_config/time_sync/etc.); coarse alignment across diverse templates.
Primary acted-on object (service/unit/interface/hostname); enables object-centric correlation across events.
Network interface identifier (ensX/lo); anchors network-state events to a concrete device.
Peer/remote IP mentioned (e.g., NTP server, gateway, upstream); aligns external dependencies.
User identity when present (e.g., CRON root); distinguishes user-initiated vs system-initiated actions.
Executed command string when present (e.g., cron task, shell command, sudo COMMAND); captures concrete action payload for behavior alignment across audit/auth/syslog.
